Defense Industrial Base · Federal Agencies

Federal Contracting
& Cybersecurity
Compliance

From CMMC certification to federal digital forensic investigations — we provide the cybersecurity expertise that government contractors and agencies require. Available as a prime or subcontractor on government project bids.

Federal Capabilities

What We Deliver

Our federal cybersecurity practice spans compliance preparation, active incident response, digital forensics for government, and bid support as a teaming subcontractor.

i.

CMMC Compliance & Assessment Preparation

We prepare DoD contractors for Cybersecurity Maturity Model Certification across all three levels — gap assessments, remediation planning, System Security Plan (SSP) development, and pre-assessment readiness reviews.

  • CMMC Level 1 self-assessment support (110 practices)
  • CMMC Level 2 C3PAO pre-assessment readiness
  • CMMC Level 3 advanced practice gap analysis
  • System Security Plan (SSP) authoring and review
  • Plan of Action & Milestones (POA&M) development
ii.

NIST 800-171 & DFARS Compliance

DFARS 252.204-7012 requires contractors handling CUI to implement NIST SP 800-171. We conduct assessments, implement required controls, and document compliance for contract vehicles and prime contractor audits.

  • 110-control NIST 800-171 gap assessment
  • CUI identification and data flow mapping
  • Contractor-facing compliance documentation packages
  • SPRS score calculation and submission support
  • Subcontractor flow-down clause compliance
iii.

Federal Digital Forensic Investigations (DFIR)

When a federal contractor or agency experiences a breach, the DFARS clause requires reporting within 72 hours. We provide rapid forensic response, malware analysis, and legally defensible incident reports that satisfy federal reporting obligations.

  • 72-hour DFARS-compliant breach notification support
  • Forensic preservation of CUI environments
  • Attribution analysis and threat actor identification
  • Classified system adjacent incident documentation
  • Federal agency liaison and chain-of-custody reporting
iv.

ITAR & Export Control Cybersecurity

Organizations that manufacture, export, or broker defense articles must secure their technical data under ITAR. We implement security controls that meet the State Department's expectations and protect controlled technical data from unauthorized access or exfiltration.

  • ITAR technical data security program design
  • Controlled technical data access controls
  • Export-controlled system network segregation
  • Foreign national access protocols
  • ITAR violation incident response
v.

Insider Threat & Counterintelligence Support

Defense contractors operating in sensitive environments face insider threat risk from employees, foreign actors, and supply chain infiltration. We conduct digital investigations, establish insider threat programs, and provide forensic support to counterintelligence teams.

  • Insider threat program development (NISPOM Chapter 2)
  • Privileged user activity monitoring architecture
  • Unauthorized data exfiltration investigation
  • Supply chain digital risk assessment
  • Counterintelligence-aligned forensic reporting
vi.

Expert Witness & Legal Support

Federal procurement disputes, False Claims Act investigations, and contractor debarment proceedings increasingly turn on digital evidence. Our principal analyst provides credible expert testimony and forensic evidence packages for government-related legal matters.

  • False Claims Act cybersecurity compliance investigations
  • DFARS breach liability documentation
  • Government contractor debarment defense support
  • GAO and Inspector General cooperation forensics
  • Federal court expert witness testimony
Teaming & Subcontracting

Join Forces on
Your Next Federal Bid

We are available as a cybersecurity subject matter expert (SME) and subcontractor on federal solicitations. Whether you're a systems integrator, defense prime, or small business teaming partner — our capabilities strengthen your proposal's technical approach and past performance profile.

Initiate Teaming Conversation Schedule Capability Briefing
We Accept Teaming Requests From:

Defense primes, systems integrators, IT service firms, and 8(a)/HUBZone/SDVOSB small businesses bidding on cybersecurity task orders, BPAs, and GWACs.

What We Bring to Your Proposal:

Technical volume authoring (cybersecurity approach), past performance in DFIR and compliance, ITAR-compliant operations, and a cleared-eligible principal analyst with Fortune 50 defense background.

Contract Vehicles & Vehicles We Support:

DHS EINSTEIN, DoD CYBERCOM task orders, CISA advisory services, BPA cybersecurity vehicles, GSA MAS, and agency-specific IDIQ cybersecurity task orders.

Bid Support We Provide:

Technical approach narrative development, past performance write-ups, management approach for cybersecurity workstreams, and oral presentation coaching for technical evaluations.

Regulatory Requirements

Federal Digital Forensic
Obligations

Federal contractors and agencies face mandatory reporting, investigation, and documentation requirements when cybersecurity incidents occur. Failure to comply carries significant legal and contractual consequences.

DFARS

72-Hour Breach Reporting

DFARS 252.204-7012 requires contractors to report cyber incidents to DoD within 72 hours of discovery. We provide forensic support and documentation that satisfies this obligation — even under time pressure.

CMMC

Audit Trail & Log Preservation

CMMC requires contractors to create, protect, and retain audit logs. We design and implement logging architectures that satisfy NIST 800-171 control AU.3.045 through AU.3.052 and survive third-party assessment.

FISMA

Continuous Monitoring

Federal agencies subject to FISMA must implement continuous monitoring programs (CONMON). We design monitoring architectures and provide ongoing analysis aligned with NIST 800-137 requirements.

FedRAMP

Cloud Security Assessment

Cloud service providers seeking FedRAMP authorization require penetration testing and vulnerability assessments. We provide assessment services aligned with the FedRAMP security assessment framework.

NIST 800-86

Federal Forensics Standards

NIST SP 800-86 establishes the federal standard for integrating forensic techniques into federal incident response. Our forensic practice is built on these guidelines — ensuring evidence is collected, preserved, and analyzed correctly.

EO 14028

Executive Order Compliance

EO 14028 mandates zero trust architecture, software bill of materials (SBOM), and enhanced logging for federal agencies and their contractors. We help organizations implement and document compliance with these requirements.

Engagement Process

How We Work with
Federal Clients

01

Capability Briefing

We review your contract, solicitation, or compliance requirement and align our services to your specific federal obligation or bid opportunity.

02

Scope & Agreement

We execute a teaming agreement, NDA, or statement of work. For cleared environments, we coordinate appropriate personnel with the right background checks.

03

Assessment & Delivery

We execute the engagement — whether a compliance assessment, forensic investigation, or technical proposal — with federal-grade documentation and chain of custody.

04

Reporting & Support

Final deliverables include a compliant report package with executive summary, technical findings, and remediation roadmap formatted for government review boards.

Work With Us

Ready to Strengthen
Your Federal Posture?

Whether you're pursuing CMMC certification, responding to a breach, or building a cybersecurity team for a government bid — we're ready to engage. All federal engagements begin with a confidential capability briefing.

Schedule a Capability Briefing Email Capabilities Statement Request
